narrative.headline: 2020 Honda Ridgeline: electrical and engine complaints lead the list
narrative.summary: Owners filed 160 complaints with NHTSA. The electrical system is the most reported area at 28%, followed closely by the engine at 21%; together they make up roughly half of all complaints. Back-over prevention (9%), forward collision avoidance (8%), and exterior lighting (7%) round out the top five. None of the reported complaints involved a crash, fire, injury, or death.

issues.title: Electrical System
issues.blurb: The most reported area: 45 reports (28%), with no crashes, fires, or injuries on record. The single largest source of owner complaints.

issues.title: Engine
issues.blurb: Second most reported: 34 reports (21%), with no crashes, fires, or injuries reported. Nearly as common as the electrical complaints.

issues.title: Back Over Prevention
issues.blurb: 14 reports (9%), with no crashes, fires, or injuries on record. A smaller but recurring complaint area.

issues.blurb: 12 reports (8%), with no crashes, fires, or injuries reported.

issues.title: Exterior Lighting
issues.blurb: 11 reports (7%), with no crashes, fires, or injuries on record. The smallest of the top five complaint areas.
